sn46.3:1.1: “Those *bhikkhus*, bhikkhus, who are accomplished in ethical conduct, accomplished in *samādhi* [*samādhisampannā*], accomplished in knowledge, accomplished in liberation, accomplished in the knowledge and vision of liberation — I say that seeing those *bhikkhus* is of great help;
sn46.3:1.2: I say that hearing those *bhikkhus* is of great help;
sn46.3:1.3: I say that approaching those *bhikkhus* is of great help;
sn46.3:1.4: I say that attending on those *bhikkhus* is of great help;
sn46.3:1.5: I say that recollecting those *bhikkhus* is of great help;
sn46.3:1.6: I say that going forth after those *bhikkhus* is of great help.
sn46.3:1.8: Having heard the *dhamma* from *bhikkhus* of that kind, one dwells withdrawn in two ways — withdrawn in body and withdrawn in mind [*citta*].
sn46.3:1.9: Dwelling thus withdrawn, one recollects that *dhamma* and thinks it over.
sn46.3:2.1: At whatever time, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu*, dwelling thus withdrawn, recollects that *dhamma* and thinks it over, at that time the *bojjhaṅga* of *sati* [*satisambojjhaṅgo*] is aroused in that *bhikkhu*;
sn46.3:2.2: at that time the *bhikkhu* develops the *bojjhaṅga* of *sati* [*satisambojjhaṅgaṁ*];
sn46.3:2.3: at that time the *bojjhaṅga* of *sati* [*satisambojjhaṅgo*] in that *bhikkhu* goes to fulfillment in development.
sn46.3:2.4: Dwelling thus with *sati*, one investigates that *dhamma* with discernment [*paññāya*], examines it, and undertakes a thorough inquiry into it.
sn46.3:3.1: At whatever time, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu*, dwelling thus with *sati*, investigates that *dhamma* with discernment [*paññāya*], examines it, and undertakes a thorough inquiry into it, at that time the *bojjhaṅga* of investigation of *dhammas* [*dhammavicayasambojjhaṅgo*] is aroused in that *bhikkhu*;
sn46.3:3.2: at that time the *bhikkhu* develops the *bojjhaṅga* of investigation of *dhammas* [*dhammavicayasambojjhaṅgaṁ*];
sn46.3:3.3: at that time the *bojjhaṅga* of investigation of *dhammas* [*dhammavicayasambojjhaṅgo*] in that *bhikkhu* goes to fulfillment in development.
sn46.3:3.4: For one investigating that *dhamma* with discernment [*paññāya*], examining it, and undertaking a thorough inquiry into it, energy becomes aroused, unsluggish.
sn46.3:4.1: At whatever time, bhikkhus, for a *bhikkhu* investigating that *dhamma* with discernment [*paññāya*], examining it, and undertaking a thorough inquiry into it, energy becomes aroused, unsluggish, at that time the *bojjhaṅga* of energy [*vīriyasambojjhaṅgo*] is aroused in that *bhikkhu*; at that time the *bhikkhu* develops the *bojjhaṅga* of energy [*vīriyasambojjhaṅgaṁ*]; at that time the *bojjhaṅga* of energy [*vīriyasambojjhaṅgo*] in that *bhikkhu* goes to fulfillment in development.
sn46.3:4.2: For one whose energy is aroused, there arises unworldly *pīti*.
sn46.3:5.1: At whatever time, bhikkhus, for a *bhikkhu* whose energy is aroused there arises unworldly *pīti*, at that time the *bojjhaṅga* of *pīti* [*pītisambojjhaṅgo*] is aroused in that *bhikkhu*; at that time the *bhikkhu* develops the *bojjhaṅga* of *pīti* [*pītisambojjhaṅgaṁ*]; at that time the *bojjhaṅga* of *pīti* [*pītisambojjhaṅgo*] in that *bhikkhu* goes to fulfillment in development.
sn46.3:5.2: For one with *pīti*, the body grows calm and the mind [*citta*] grows calm.
sn46.3:6.1: At whatever time, bhikkhus, for a *bhikkhu* with *pīti* the body grows calm and the mind [*citta*] grows calm, at that time the *bojjhaṅga* of tranquility [*passaddhisambojjhaṅgo*] is aroused in that *bhikkhu*; at that time the *bhikkhu* develops the *bojjhaṅga* of tranquility [*passaddhisambojjhaṅgaṁ*]; at that time the *bojjhaṅga* of tranquility [*passaddhisambojjhaṅgo*] in that *bhikkhu* goes to fulfillment in development.
sn46.3:6.2: For one whose body is tranquil and who is happy, the mind [*citta*] becomes settled in *samādhi* [*samādhiyati*].
sn46.3:7.1: At whatever time, bhikkhus, for a *bhikkhu* whose body is tranquil and who is happy, the mind [*citta*] becomes settled in *samādhi* [*samādhiyati*], at that time the *bojjhaṅga* of *samādhi* [*samādhisambojjhaṅgo*] is aroused in that *bhikkhu*; at that time the *bhikkhu* develops the *bojjhaṅga* of *samādhi* [*samādhisambojjhaṅgaṁ*]; at that time the *bojjhaṅga* of *samādhi* [*samādhisambojjhaṅgo*] in that *bhikkhu* goes to fulfillment in development.
sn46.3:7.2: One then carefully looks on at that mind [*citta*] thus endowed with *samādhi* [*samāhitaṁ*].
sn46.3:8.1: At whatever time, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* carefully looks on at that mind [*citta*] thus endowed with *samādhi* [*samāhitaṁ*], at that time the *bojjhaṅga* of equanimity [*upekkhā*] [*upekkhāsambojjhaṅgo*] is aroused in that *bhikkhu*; at that time the *bhikkhu* develops the *bojjhaṅga* of equanimity [*upekkhā*] [*upekkhāsambojjhaṅgaṁ*]; at that time the *bojjhaṅga* of equanimity [*upekkhā*] [*upekkhāsambojjhaṅgo*] in that *bhikkhu* goes to fulfillment in development.
sn46.3:9.1: When, bhikkhus, these seven *bojjhaṅgas* have been developed and made much of in this way, seven fruits and seven benefits can be expected.
sn46.3:9.10: When, bhikkhus, these seven *bojjhaṅgas* have been developed and made much of in this way, these seven fruits and seven benefits can be expected.”
sn46.3:9.2: Which seven fruits and seven benefits?
sn46.3:9.3: In this very life itself, before death, one attains final knowledge.
sn46.3:9.4: If one does not attain final knowledge in this very life itself, before death, then one attains final knowledge at the time of death.
sn46.3:9.5: If one does not attain final knowledge in this very life itself, before death, and does not attain final knowledge at the time of death, then with the destruction of the five lower fetters, one is one who attains nibbāna in the interval.
sn46.3:9.6: If one does not attain final knowledge in this very life itself, before death, and does not attain final knowledge at the time of death, and is not one who attains nibbāna in the interval through the destruction of the five lower fetters, then with the destruction of the five lower fetters, one is one who attains nibbāna upon arrival.
sn46.3:9.7: If one does not attain final knowledge in this very life itself, before death, and does not attain final knowledge at the time of death, and is not one who attains nibbāna in the interval through the destruction of the five lower fetters, and is not one who attains nibbāna upon arrival through the destruction of the five lower fetters, then with the destruction of the five lower fetters, one is one who attains nibbāna without *saṅkhāra* [*asaṅkhāraparinibbāyī*].
sn46.3:9.8: If one does not attain final knowledge in this very life itself, before death, and does not attain final knowledge at the time of death, and is not one who attains nibbāna in the interval through the destruction of the five lower fetters, and is not one who attains nibbāna upon arrival through the destruction of the five lower fetters, and is not one who attains nibbāna without *saṅkhāra* [*asaṅkhāraparinibbāyī*] through the destruction of the five lower fetters, then with the destruction of the five lower fetters, one is one who attains nibbāna with *saṅkhāra* [*sasaṅkhāraparinibbāyī*].
sn46.3:9.9: If one does not attain final knowledge in this very life itself, before death, and does not attain final knowledge at the time of death, and is not one who attains nibbāna in the interval through the destruction of the five lower fetters, and is not one who attains nibbāna upon arrival through the destruction of the five lower fetters, and is not one who attains nibbāna without *saṅkhāra* [*asaṅkhāraparinibbāyī*] through the destruction of the five lower fetters, and is not one who attains nibbāna with *saṅkhāra* [*sasaṅkhāraparinibbāyī*] through the destruction of the five lower fetters, then with the destruction of the five lower fetters, one is upstream-going, headed for Akaniṭṭha.
